titleStmt.title: SII 40.113: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 113. (A.R. No. B. 92 of 1930.) Mōrjamāpāḍu, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a stone set up near the fresh water well in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1562, Vikrama, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Wednesday, corresponding to 1640 A.D., July 22.

States that a certain Timmappa, son of Sanggaya and grandson of Rēvūri Tiruṁalayya of the Viśvakarmakula and the Kāśyapa-gōtra, dug a well and constructed steps and installed the shrines of Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara and Vināyaka to the east of the well of Mōrijampāḍu. Aṁbeya son of Gōpāla was a witness to this record.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīnīlakaṁṭhāyanamaḥ . śrī veṁkaṭēśvarāyanamaḥ
svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavarṣaṁ
bulu 1562 Agunēṭi vikrama nāma saṁvvatsara śrāvaṇa
śuddha 15 bu viśvakarma kulōdbhava mēghavāhana kanakakuṁḍḍa
rā(lā)bharaṇa kāḷikādēvi kamalēśvara divya śrī pādapadmārādhaka kāśyapagō
tṛlaina rēvūri tirumalaya pautṛluṁnnu saṁggaya mu
salammagāri putṛlaina timmappa mōrijaṁppāṭi sthāḷāna
nu naḍabāvi tavviṁcci sōpānālu gaṭṭiṁci bāvilōnu
tūrppubhāgāna nīlakaṁṭhēśvaraniki dēvaḷaṁ kaṭṭiṁci Ā
mīda vināyakuni dēvaḷaṁ kaṭṭiṁcci tama vubhaya ku
lāla vāriki puṇyaṁgānu Ācaṁdrārkasthāyigānu
pratiṣṭa śeyiṁccenu śivārppaṇamastu śrī śrī śrī
jeyunū . gopāḷuni Aṁcceya vrālu .
Digital edition of SII 40.113 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
